findings.evidence: `GridDiagram.OColumnOfRow` and `XColumnOfRow` are just `G.O.columnOfRow` and `G.X.columnOfRow` at `Diagram.lean:599-603`; `GridState.rotate` and `rotate_apply` already exist for arbitrary states at `Rotation.lean:83-90`; the new proofs at lines 49-62 repeat the same argument for O and X.
findings.fix: First add the general theorem `GridState.columnOfRow_rotate (x : GridState n) (r : Fin n) : x.rotate.columnOfRow r = (x.columnOfRow r.rev).rev`, then derive the O/X lemmas by `simp [GridDiagram.OColumnOfRow, GridDiagram.XColumnOfRow]`.
findings.issue: `OColumnOfRow_rotate` and `XColumnOfRow_rotate` are duplicate specializations of the same `GridState.columnOfRow` rotation formula.

summary: The rotation lookup API is stated one level too specifically: it duplicates the same GridState fact for O and X markings instead of exposing the natural state-level theorem.
